ocr.text: The data presented in Table 1 elucidates the role of specific miRNAs in the context of lung and blood tissues, with a particular
focus on miR-30b. This miRNA has been identified as a significant regulator of various biological functions, which may have
implications for therapeutic strategies in respiratory diseases and systemic conditions.
miRNA  Lung  Blood  Main regulated functions
miR-30b  0.27  Intercellular adhesion, protein repair, NFkB activation, cell cycle, EGF activation, stem cell recruitment, multidrug resistance
miR-30c  0.47  Intercellular adhesion, protein repair, NFkB activation, cell cycle, EGF activation, stem cell recruitment, multidrug resistance
miR-101b  1.86  COX activation
miR-125b  0.31  Oncogene ERBB regulation, vitamin D receptor, inflammation
miR-206  0.27  Myoblast differentiation and proliferation
miR-291a  0.47  Differentiation of embryonic stem cells
miR-344b  4.83  Cell plasticity
miR-344h  2.05  Cell plasticity
miR-483  5.45  Epithelial-mesenchymal transition, prometastatic fuction (RhoGDI1 and ALCAM regulation), angiogenesis-inhibiting factor
miR-711  0.1  0.52  Tissue fibrosis
In the table, the expression level of miR-30b in lung tissue is quantified at 0.27, indicating a relatively lower expression
compared to other miRNAs that may play a more prominent role in lung pathology. Notably, the column labeled "Main
Regulated Functions" highlights critical processes influenced by miR-30b, including intercellular adhesion and protein repair,
which are essential for maintaining cellular integrity and function. Furthermore, its involvement in NFkB activation and the cell
cycle underscores the potential for miR-30b to contribute to the modulation of inflammatory responses and cellular
proliferation. Given the interplay between these processes and various disease states, understanding the regulatory impact of
miR-30b in both lung and blood contexts is vital for advancing our knowledge of its functional roles and potential as a
biomarker or therapeutic target in clinical applications.
